rationale: The cited paper is used in the citing paper's theory-building, not just in a generic literature list. In the theoretical background, it is directly invoked to support the key premise that construal level changes how consumers interpret framed stimuli, which motivates the predicted specificity x construal interaction. In the general discussion, the citing authors return to Cheema and Patrick (2008) to interpret their findings as consistent with prior mindset-dependent framing effects.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the core idea from Cheema and Patrick (2008) that mind-set/construal moderates responses to alternative frames. It transfers that logic from promotion-window framing to savings-goal framing (specific vs nonspecific goals) to explain differences in anticipated and actual saving success.
